channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Rescue mission launches to save NASA telescope that's falling back to Earth thanks to solar storms - PBS
item.description:
1. Rescue mission launches to save NASA telescope that's falling back to Earth thanks to solar storms  PBS
  2. Daring rescue mission launches to save a 3,200-pound NASA observatory from an untimely end  CNN
  3. Nasa launches mission to save falling space telescope  BBC
  4. Teams Make Contact With Spacecraft Set to Boost NASA’s Swift  NASA Science (.gov)
  5. NASA's $30 million telescope rescue mission  Axios

item.title: 'It's more than a hope, it's a guarantee': The Rubin Observatory's 10-year movie of the universe is about to 'blow our minds,' chief scientist says - Live Science
item.description:
1. 'It's more than a hope, it's a guarantee': The Rubin Observatory's 10-year movie of the universe is about to 'blow our minds,' chief scientist says  Live Science

  3. A giant telescope goes on a decade-long search for dark matter  The Washington Post
  4. A Dazzling Census of Our Cosmos Begins  The New York Times
  5. LSST begins full operations with key contributions from Japanese researchers and engineers  Phys.org

item.title: Hobbit-like human relatives may have been on a less advanced evolutionary path - CNN
item.description:

  4. ‘Hobbit’ hominins scavenged meat left over by Komodo dragons  New Scientist

  4. 'Machine-gun sun' could bring auroras to more than a dozen states this Independence Day weekend  Live Science

item.title: 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life - Fox News
item.description:
1. 'Milestone': Scientists claim to build synthetic cell, raising concerns in step toward artificial life  Fox News
  2. Scientists React to the Lab-Made, Yet Lifelike, SpudCell  The New York Times

  4. Scientists Say They've Made Cells That Feed, Grow and Reproduce, Bringing Them One Step Closer to Building Life From Scratch  Smithsonian Magazine
  5. What is 'SpudCell'? Arguably the greatest bioengineering feat yet  New Scientist

item.title: 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star - Space
item.description:
1. 'Stellar death is not the end': James Webb Space Telescope glimpses the fate of the solar system in a weird exoplanet orbiting a dead star  Space
  2. Aerosols and hydrocarbons in the atmosphere of a white dwarf planet  Nature
  3. ‘Bizarre’ planet orbiting dead star may preview fate of our solar system  CNN
  4. NASA’s Webb Studies How Planet Survived Death of its Star  NASA Science (.gov)
  5. Earth's future revisited as scientists find giant planet survived death of its sun  Newsweek

item.title: Potentially Habitable Super-Earth Found Just 25 Light-Years Away - ScienceAlert
item.description:
1. Potentially Habitable Super-Earth Found Just 25 Light-Years Away  ScienceAlert
  2. Astronomers discover a potentially habitable planet just 25 light-years away. 'This one is exciting'  Space
  3. UC Irvine astronomers discover a new Earth-like exoplanet  UC Irvine News
  4. Nearby 'Super Earth' may be a better candidate for life than previously thought  Phys.org
  5. A world just 25 light-years away sits in the narrow zone where liquid water could exist on its surface — and astronomers at UC Irvine have now revised its mass down to just 2.3 times Earth’s, making it look less like a gas-shrouded mini-Neptune and more like s  Space Daily

item.title: Could humans someday explore Saturn's moon Titan, or will humanoid robots do it for us? - Space
item.description:
1. Could humans someday explore Saturn's moon Titan, or will humanoid robots do it for us?  Space
  2. Titan, Saturn’s Largest Moon, Could Become a ‘Gas Station’ for Deep Space Missions  ZME Science
  3. Methane, natural fuel lakes, ice, and a nitrogen-rich atmosphere make Titan central to NASA's plans for the first "space fuel station" to speed up missions beyond Mars.  CPG Click Oil and Gas
  4. Titan’s Resources and their Utilization  Astrobiology Web
  5. ⛽ Titan, the interplanetary gas station  Techno-Science.net

item.title: Physicists Simulated a Black Hole in a Lab. Then It Started to 'Evaporate'. - ScienceAlert
item.description:

item.title: Sea level doesn’t rise at the same rate everywhere – we mapped where Antarctica’s ice melt would have the biggest impact - Yahoo News UK
item.description:
1. Sea level doesn’t rise at the same rate everywhere – we mapped where Antarctica’s ice melt would have the biggest impact  Yahoo News UK

  3. Scientists Sound the Alarm: Antarctic Discovery Is More Disturbing Than Anyone Feared  Futura, le média qui explore le monde
  4. Twice a day the tide lifts a glacier the size of Florida, and satellites just caught warm seawater slipping through the door it opens in a way no one thought was possible  ecoportal.net
  5. Antarctica’s future is uncertain—but the next few decades are not  yourweather.co.uk

item.title: The word algebra comes from the title of a book called Al-Jabr, written around the year 820 by a Persian mathematician named Muhammad ibn Musa al-Khwarizmi at the House of Wisdom in Baghdad — and the word algorithm comes from the Latinized versio - Space Daily

item.title: Fossil jaws hidden inside Cretaceous rocks have revealed that 100 million years ago, some octopuses grew into giant predators — possibly longer than a bus and more fearsome than any octopus alive today. - Space Daily

item.title: A rare meteorite found in the Sahara may be a surviving shard of a lost Moon-sized world — one that formed beside the young Sun, followed a path unlike Earth or Mars, and was smashed apart while the planets were still being built. - Space Daily

item.title: In November, a spacecraft that has spent eight years falling toward the Sun will finally settle into orbit around Mercury, a planet we have seen up close fewer times than almost any other in the solar system. - Space Daily
item.description:
1. In November, a spacecraft that has spent eight years falling toward the Sun will finally settle into orbit around Mercury, a planet we have seen up close fewer times than almost any other in the solar system.  Space Daily

  4. Electric blue thrusters propelling BepiColombo to Mercury  European Space Agency

item.title: Voyager 1 launched in 1977 on a four-year mission, and in November 2026, nearly half a century later, it will become the first object ever to reach one light-day from Earth — so distant that a signal now takes a full 24 hours to cross the emptiness back home - 19FortyFive
item.description:
1. Voyager 1 launched in 1977 on a four-year mission, and in November 2026, nearly half a century later, it will become the first object ever to reach one light-day from Earth — so distant that a signal now takes a full 24 hours to cross the emptiness back home  19FortyFive
  2. Voyager 1 to reach 1 light-day from Earth on November 18  EarthSky
  3. Voyager 1 isn't dying because anything broke — after almost 50 years it's simply running out of power, and NASA is shutting off its instruments one by one until the signal goes silent.  19FortyFive
  4. A voyage to infinity and beyond: Voyager's remarkable journey continues  Vision IAS
  5. Humanity’s Farthest Signal: Voyager 1 Hits One Light-Day  Indianweb2.com
